decisions.reasoning: Strong Azure fundamentals (40% YoY growth, commercial bookings +112%, remaining performance obligation $392B) support long-term thesis despite -8.7% unrealized loss and flat SMA alignment. Bear risks: $190B AI capex not yet monetized, Copilot adoption below 15M seats, FCF pressure. Position is small ($1,165). Analyst consensus PT is $528 (35% upside). Watch $380 for break-below trim trigger.

sources.headline: Microsoft Q1 FY2026: Azure +40% YoY, commercial bookings +112%, revenue $77.7B +18% YoY
Microsoft stock trailing market in 2026 - down 17% YTD vs S&P +7%, analyst PT $528.88
Microsoft $190B AI bet is both the bull case and bear case - Copilot adoption risk

decisions.invalidation: Azure growth slows below 30% YoY, or stock closes below $380 on volume without recovery within 2 sessions

decisions.reasoning: Q1 FY27 results exceptional: revenue +85% YoY to $81.6B, Data Center +92% YoY. Near-term weakness driven by Broadcom Q3 margin compression (EBITDA stepping to 67.8%) and sector-wide selloff (AMD -7.3%, Intel -8.5% on June 16), not NVDA-specific. $25B bond offering on June 15 signals management confidence. Blackwell architecture continues hyperscaler adoption. Semiconductor stocks recovering June 17.

sources.headline: NVIDIA gains 3.5% after announcing $25 billion bond offering
Semiconductor stocks recover after Tuesday decline: NVDA -2.4%, AMD -7.3%, Intel -8.5%
After Nvidia, Broadcoms earnings are next - margin compression signals AI capex cooling concern

decisions.invalidation: NVDA Q2 FY27 revenue guidance misses $43B consensus, or hyperscaler capex guidance cuts next earnings cycle

decisions.reasoning: Bought June 16, less than 24 hours old per no-churn rule. Only symbol with SMA long signal (fast $720.68 > slow $719.69, price $731.26 above both). AI-driven tech earnings story intact. Fed held 3.50-3.75% as expected with no shock. Down -1.56% from entry but technical picture positive. QQQ up 11% over 30 days prior to today driven by AI earnings.

sources.headline: QQQ closed near all-time highs at $744, up 11% over 30 days driven by AI earnings
Fed maintained rates at 3.50-3.75% in Kevin Warsh first FOMC - eliminated projected rate cut

decisions.invalidation: QQQ breaks and closes below fast SMA ($720) on sustained volume, or major tech earnings miss

decisions.reasoning: Only profitable position (+2.29%). Retail sales surged +0.9% in May vs +0.5% expected, confirming consumer resilience. S&P 500 rose 0.33% to 7,536 on June 17. Fed hold was expected; market absorbed it without shock. Would add but gross long is 91.1% of equity with only ~$375 headroom before 95% cap - insufficient for meaningful addition.

sources.headline: S&P 500 rose 0.33% to 7,536 ahead of Fed decision; retail sales surged 0.9% in May beat
Fed rate decision June 17: rates held 3.50-3.75%, dot plot eliminates 2026 rate cut

decisions.invalidation: Retail spending reverses sharply or tariff-driven inflation re-accelerates above 5% forcing Fed to hike

summary: First session (journal empty). Held all 4 positions - no trades. 91% gross long leaves no meaningful buying capacity. Day P/L -$18.03 (-0.19%). Fed eliminated 2026 rate cut projection - headwind for growth names.
open_theses: NVDA: secular AI data center demand confirmed by Q1 FY27 +85% YoY; holding through Broadcom-driven sector noise; watch $200 as trim trigger
SPY: economic resilience intact - retail sales +0.9% beat, consumer strong; only green position; would add if gross long drops below 85%
QQQ: long SMA signal, AI tech story supported by MSFT Azure +40% and NVDA Blackwell; monitor for SMA crossover reversal
MSFT: fundamental case strong (Azure 40% YoY, bookings +112%) but stock underperforming market; consider trim if closes below $380; watch next Azure quarterly for deceleration signal
